sections.paragraph:
(from the root יָדַד No. II), m.-

(1) beloved, a friend, Isa 5:1. יְדִיד יְהֹוָה beloved by the Lord, Psa 127:2 applied to Benjamin, Deu 33:12 in pl. to the Israelites, Psa 60:7 108:7 .

(2) pleasant, lovely, Psa 84:2. Plur. יְדִידֹות charms. Psa 45:1, שִׁיר יְדִידֹות “a pleasant song.” Others, “a song of loves,” i.e. an epithalamium. (Syr. ܝܰܕܝܺܕܐܳ beloved.)
